tweets.text:
It’s not that I *want* him to collapse.

It’s that when you dive deeper into the ponzi, I believe Strategy inevitably has to eventually deleverage to survive.

They got greedy and made themselves vulnerable.

Worst case scenario is they don’t deleverage now, things bounce for a bit, they grow larger and make the fallout even worse when it eventually does happen.

E.g. most people agree that FTX collapsing when it did saved the industry from something that could have been worse if they continued to act as reckless as they were and grew even larger

thread_tweets.text:
I was wrong for way longer than I should have been.

Wish it wouldn’t have taken seeing obvious lies and shifting of goalposts from Saylor and Strategy to open my eyes.

I’m trying to do what I can to help people open their eyes to this shit before it does get worse.

Still a huge believer in Bitcoin and the industry as a whole which does make me incredibly bullish down the road, but this is a massive ponzi that inevitably needs to be deleveraged.

I’d rather deal with the pain now rather than later giving it the chance to grow larger and see the fallout be even worse.

tweets.text:
Do you realize why Strategy was forced to buy back $1.5B of converts for $1.38B (a nice little discount)?

In the past, these converts would be above their put price and just become common shares of MSTR, which was a win for the lender and a win for Strategy because they didn’t have to find cash to repay the debt.

Their converts are now so far out of the money they have no hope of seeing the majority of them actually convert.

This is something they’ve never had to do at this scale before.

And they have another $6B of these notes they have to pay off by the end of 2028.

This is cash that otherwise could have been used to pay off dividends to preferred shares had the stock performed better and the premium not seen such compression already.

Now that cash that would have been available has to be funded from somewhere else to keep the pref dividend payments alive… and the best options left are selling Bitcoin or more common MSTR shares at a compressed premium.

Both are dilutive to MSTR holders.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: @CryptoKaleo @CryptoParadyme They just bought back $1.5b of converts, they already did deleverage.
tweets.full_text:
Do you realize why Strategy was forced to buy back $1.5B of converts for $1.38B (a nice little discount)?

In the past, these converts would be above their put price and just become common shares of MSTR, which was a win for the lender and a win for Strategy because they didn’t have to find cash to repay the debt.

Their converts are now so far out of the money they have no hope of seeing the majority of them actually convert.

This is something they’ve never had to do at this scale before.

And they have another $6B of these notes they have to pay off by the end of 2028.

This is cash that otherwise could have been used to pay off dividends to preferred shares had the stock performed better and the premium not seen such compression already.

Now that cash that would have been available has to be funded from somewhere else to keep the pref dividend payments alive… and the best options left are selling Bitcoin or more common MSTR shares at a compressed premium.

Both are dilutive to MSTR holders.

tweets.text:
If you genuinely felt like you could succeed in anything you do with enough effort, you'd work 10X harder.

Unironically, if you worked 10X harder, you most likely would succeed.

And that's what ppl call the law of manifestation/attraction or whatever.

tweets.text:
When you look at whats going on socially in America and Europe it feels like we're being driven into seeing everything black and white (no pun intended) and the people that see things as more of a grey, which in reality  tend to be correct most of the time, those ppl are either staying quiet for fear of upsetting both sides or being drowned out by the louder voices because due to our tribalistic nature it feels good to be on the battlefield.

What's going on this week reminds me in a way of the tipping point reached when the George Floyd video was released. Ppl forget just days before there was a video of a black man in central park minding his business walking his dog and this white woman called the cops and said he was trying to hurt her, it was bad. Ppl were really upset when that came out and u combine that with the stress of an early covid shutdown and the country became a powder keg.

In Europe we just had Henry Nowak and that horrible beheading in succession and in America Belton and Anthony cases. It's weird because out of the two personally I think that Belton's shooter not being charged is by far the worse miscarriage of justice when in comparison to ppl yelling free Karmelo. 

Anyway we see whats going on in Ireland and GB right now and I wouldn't be surprised to see something similar happen in Ameirca soon. Combine all this tension with looming ww3+ai threats and its arguably an even worse societal climate than what we experienced in 2020

I don't really have the answers, that needs to come from leadership on both sides but at the very least I guess if you see things grey you gotta speak up. Be safe and be good to each other we gotta find a way to make this work because realistically what is the alternatives here?
